passage:
In order to "change lives for the better" and reduce "dependency,"
George Osbome, Chancellor of the Exchequer, introduced the "upfront
work search" scheme. Only if the jobless arrive at the jobcentre with
a CV, register for online job search, and start looking for work will
they be eligible for benefit - and then they should report weekly rather
than fortnightly. What could be more reasonable?

More apparent reasonableness followed. There will now be a seven-day
wait for the jobseeker's allowance. "Those first few days should be
spent looking for work, not looking to sign on," he
claimed. "We're doing these things because we know they help people stay
off benefits and help those on benefits get into work faster." Help?
Really? On first hearing, this was the socially concerned chancellor,
trying to change lives for the better, complete with "reforms" to an
obviously indulgent system that demands too little effort from the newly
unemployed to find work, and subsidises laziness. What motivated him, we
were to understand, was his zeal for "fundamental fairness" - protecting
the taxpayer, controlling spending and ensuring that only the most
deserving claimants received their benefits.

Losing a job is hurting: you don't skip down to the jobcentre with a
song in your heart, delighted at the prospect of doubling your income
from the generous state. It is financially terrifying, psychologically
embarrassing and you know that support is minimal and extraordinarily
hard to get. You are now not wanted; you support is minimal and
extraordinarily hard to get. You are now not wanted; you are now
excluded from the work environment that offers purpose and structure in
your life. Worse, the crucial income to feed yourself and your family
and pay the bills has disappeared. Ask anyone newly unemployed what they
want and the answer is always: a job.

But in Osborneland, your first instinct is to fall into dependency - permanent dependency if you can get it - supported by a state only too
ready to indulge your falsehood. It is as though 20 years of ever-tougher reforms of the job search and benefit administration system
never happened. The principle of British welfare is no longer that you
can insure yourself against the risk of unemployment and receive
unconditional payments if the disaster happens. Even the very phrase
"jobseeker's allowance" is about redefining the
unemployed as a "jobseeker" who had no fundamental right to a benefit he
or she has earned through making national insurance contributions.
Instead, the claimant receives a time-limited "allowance," conditional
on actively seeking a job; no entitlement and no insurance, at £71.70 a
week, one of the least generous in the EU.

questions.questionText: George Osborne's scheme was intended to ____.
questions.question: George Osborne's scheme was intended to ____.

options.A: provide the unemployed with easier access to benefits
options.B: encourage jobseekers' active engagement in job seeking
options.C: motivate the unemployed to report voluntarily
options.D: guarantee jobseekers' legitimate right to benefits

questions.questionText: The phrase "to sign on" (Line 3, Para. 2) most probably means ____.
questions.question: The phrase "to sign on" (Line 3, Para. 2) most probably means ____.

options.A: to check on the availability of jobs at the jobcentre
options.B: to accept the government's restrictions on the allowance
options.C: to register for an allowance from the government
options.D: to attend a governmental job-training program

questions.questionText: What prompted the chancellor to develop his scheme?
questions.question: What prompted the chancellor to develop his scheme?

options.A: A desire to secure a better life for all.
options.B: An eagerness to protect the unemployed.
options.C: An urge to be generous to the claimants.
options.D: A passion to ensure fairness for taxpayers.

questions.explanation: 定位：P2: What motivated him ... was his zeal for fundamental fairness - protecting the taxpayer, controlling spending...。题干的 prompted 对应原文 What motivated him，真正动因是保护纳税人、控制支出的 fairness，对应 D。
questions.trapAnalysis: 干扰项主要来自局部词复现、选项顺序错位或对原文态度的误读。
questions.commonMistake: 常见错因：只看表面关键词，没有结合转折、作者立场和上下文判断。
questions.location: P2: What motivated him ... was his zeal for fundamental fairness - protecting the taxpayer, controlling spending...
questions.thinkingMethod: 先回到题干定位句，再把选项和原文做同义替换、逻辑和态度核对。

examinerThinking.sourceSentence: P2: What motivated him ... was his zeal for fundamental fairness - protecting the taxpayer, controlling spending...
examinerThinking.trap: 干扰项主要来自局部词复现、选项顺序错位或对原文态度的误读。
examinerThinking.correctLogic: 题干的 prompted 对应原文 What motivated him，真正动因是保护纳税人、控制支出的 fairness，对应 D。
examinerThinking.nextTimeStrategy: 先回到题干定位句，再把选项和原文做同义替换、逻辑和态度核对。

questions.questionText: According to Paragraph 3, being unemployed makes one feel ____.
questions.question: According to Paragraph 3, being unemployed makes one feel ____.

questions.questionText: To which of the following would the author most probably agree?
questions.question: To which of the following would the author most probably agree?

options.A: The British welfare system indulges jobseekers' laziness.
options.B: Osborne's reforms will reduce the risk of unemployment.
options.C: The jobseekers' allowance has met their actual needs.
options.D: Unemployment benefits should not be made conditional.

questions.explanation: 定位：Last paragraph: receive unconditional payments; instead ... allowance, conditional on actively seeking a job; no entitlement and no insurance.。文章后段批判把失业救济改写为有条件的 allowance，作者更可能赞同失业救济不应被条件化，对应 D。
questions.trapAnalysis: 干扰项主要来自局部词复现、选项顺序错位或对原文态度的误读。
questions.commonMistake: 常见错因：只看表面关键词，没有结合转折、作者立场和上下文判断。
questions.location: Last paragraph: receive unconditional payments; instead ... allowance, conditional on actively seeking a job; no entitlement and no insurance.
questions.thinkingMethod: 先回到题干定位句，再把选项和原文做同义替换、逻辑和态度核对。

examinerThinking.sourceSentence: Last paragraph: receive unconditional payments; instead ... allowance, conditional on actively seeking a job; no entitlement and no insurance.
examinerThinking.trap: 干扰项主要来自局部词复现、选项顺序错位或对原文态度的误读。
examinerThinking.correctLogic: 文章后段批判把失业救济改写为有条件的 allowance，作者更可能赞同失业救济不应被条件化，对应 D。
